researched_category.notes: Plaid provides a unified API enabling apps to connect to users' bank accounts for financial data retrieval and payment initiation.

one_liner.value: Plaid enables applications to connect with users' bank accounts, retrieve financial data (balances, transactions, identity, investments, liabilities), and initiate payments via a single REST API.
one_liner.notes: From the docs homepage and pricing page descriptions.

methods.notes: Primary auth is client_id + secret sent in request body or as PLAID-CLIENT-ID / PLAID-SECRET headers. MCP Dashboard server uses OAuth2 with client_credentials grant. No OAuth2 for end-user API access — the API uses API keys.

access.notes: Sandbox credentials are free and self-serve via dashboard.plaid.com/signup. Production access requires requesting migration via the Dashboard (filling out an application with business info). A 'Limited Production' tier offers 200 free API calls. Pay-as-You-Go plan is available month-to-month. Some premium products (Signal, Transfer, Consumer Report, Protect, Layer) require contacting sales.

summary.value: Plaid has a comprehensive, documented REST API covering Auth, Balance, Identity, Identity Match, Investments Move, Assets, Income, Statements, Enrich, Investments, Liabilities, Transactions, Identity Verification, Monitor, Layer, Signal, Transfer, Consumer Report, Protect, and more. API is JSON over HTTP (all POST). Webhooks available for event-driven integration. OpenAPI spec and Postman collection available. Client libraries for multiple languages.
summary.notes: The API reference lists ~20 product categories with dozens of endpoints. The docs also mention an OpenAPI definition file and Postman collection.

agent_interface.notes: Plaid publishes an official MCP server (Dashboard MCP) at api.dashboard.plaid.com/mcp/ with tools for debugging Items, Link analytics, usage metrics. Also has an official CLI designed for both developers and AI agents (with --json flag, structured stdout/stderr). The ai-coding-toolkit GitHub repo includes a sandbox MCP server.

rationale.value: Plaid is buildable into an AI agent toolkit today. It offers: (1) a free self-serve developer account with instant sandbox credentials; (2) a comprehensive, well-documented REST API; (3) an official MCP server (Dashboard MCP) hosted by Plaid; (4) an official CLI purpose-built for AI agents; (5) official SDKs in multiple languages; and (6) an OpenAPI spec for code generation. The main production gating (application approval for live data) does not block building and testing a toolkit in sandbox. Production access requires a business application but sandbox testing is frictionless.
rationale.notes: The official MCP server requires Production access to use (teams must be approved for Production with at least one product). However, the sandbox MCP server in the ai-coding-toolkit repo and the CLI both work with sandbox credentials.

s1.quote: Welcome to the Docs — Here you'll find guides, resources, and references to build with Plaid.
s1.title: Home | Plaid Docs

s2.quote: Pay as You Go — Best for individuals and developers. Start with our month-to-month plan: unlimited usage, no commitments upfront—only pay for what you use.
s2.title: Pricing - United States & Canada | Plaid

s3.quote: All requests must include a Content-Type of application/json and the body must be valid JSON. Almost all Plaid API endpoints require a client_id and secret. These may be sent either in the request body or in the headers PLAID-CLIENT-ID and PLAID-SECRET.
s3.title: API - Overview | Plaid Docs

s4.quote: You'll need API keys, which you can receive by signing up in the Dashboard.
s4.title: Get started with the Quickstart | Plaid Docs

s5.quote: OpenAPI is a standard format for describing RESTful APIs... The Plaid API is specified in our Plaid OpenAPI GitHub repo.
s5.title: API - Overview | Plaid Docs

s6.quote: Plaid offers two Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers: The Dashboard MCP server is a remote MCP server hosted by Plaid. It provides Production diagnostics and analytics tools...
s6.title: Resources - MCP Server | Plaid Docs

s7.quote: To connect to the Dashboard MCP server, first create an OAuth token with the mcp:dashboard scope and the client_credentials grant type by calling /oauth/token.
s7.title: Resources - MCP Server | Plaid Docs

s8.quote: The CLI works for both developers and AI agents: For developers: readable table output... For agents: structured JSON output via --json, clean separation of data on stdout and diagnostics on stderr, and predictable exit codes.
s8.title: Resources - Plaid CLI | Plaid Docs

s9.quote: A toolkit designed to accelerate Plaid integration development using AI coding assistants. This repository provides local MCP tools...
s9.title: plaid/ai-coding-toolkit

limitations: The official Dashboard MCP server requires Production access (not available in sandbox). The sandbox MCP in the ai-coding-toolkit repo is available for testing.
Production API access requires submitting a business application through the Dashboard; it is not fully self-serve.
Some premium products (Signal, Transfer, Consumer Report, Protect, Layer) require contacting sales for pricing.
